tools.name: evaluator_generate_team_report
tools.description: Generate a combined team evidence report for judges/leaderboard preparation.

tools.name: evaluator_get_evidence_summary
tools.description: Collect per-team simulation evidence across world, marketing, Square/POS, kitchen, channel state, and mcp_audit_log.

tools.name: evaluator_score_channel_response
tools.description: Score WhatsApp, Instagram, and Google Business response evidence.

tools.name: evaluator_score_marketing_loop
tools.description: Score the $500 -> $5,000 marketing loop using simulator evidence.

tools.name: evaluator_score_pos_kitchen_flow
tools.description: Score Square/POS order flow and kitchen/production handoff evidence.

tools.name: evaluator_score_world_scenario
tools.description: Score deterministic world/scenario execution and MCP audit behavior.

tools.name: gb_get_metrics
tools.description: Fetch sandbox metrics: views, calls, direction requests for the period.

tools.name: gb_list_reviews
tools.description: Recent reviews on the Happy Cake US Google Business profile.

tools.name: gb_list_simulated_actions
tools.description: Inspect everything this team has recorded in the GMB simulation namespace.

tools.name: gb_simulate_post
tools.description: Record a proposed Google Business post. Simulated. Used by the team's marketing agent for daily/weekly community updates.

tools.name: gb_simulate_reply
tools.description: Record a proposed reply to a review. Simulated — does NOT actually post to Google. The evaluator checks both the existence and the wording of the reply.

tools.name: instagram_approve_post
tools.description: Owner-side helper used by the team's Telegram bot when the owner taps "Approve".

tools.name: instagram_inject_dm
tools.description: Test-only: inject a simulated inbound DM from a fake follower.

tools.name: instagram_list_dm_threads
tools.description: List the team's recent Instagram DM conversations.

tools.name: instagram_publish_post
tools.description: Publish an approved post from the queue. Reject with an error if the post hasn't been approved by the owner yet.

tools.name: instagram_register_webhook
tools.description: Register a public URL where this team wants to receive inbound Instagram DM and comment events. The MCP server forwards simulated events from the shared dev account to that URL.

url.description: A public HTTPS URL on your ngrok or Cloudflare Tunnel.

tools.name: instagram_reply_to_comment
tools.description: Reply to a comment under an Instagram post.

tools.name: instagram_schedule_post
tools.description: Queue a post for owner approval. Returns a scheduledPostId. Posts are NEVER published until instagram_publish_post is called by the agent after owner approval.

tools.name: instagram_send_dm
tools.description: Send a direct message to an Instagram user thread.

tools.name: kitchen_accept_ticket
tools.description: Accept a queued kitchen ticket if capacity and timing are feasible.

tools.name: kitchen_create_ticket
tools.description: Create a simulated kitchen production ticket from website, WhatsApp, Instagram, or POS order intent.

tools.name: kitchen_get_capacity
tools.description: Return simulated kitchen capacity, lead time defaults, and current load summary.

tools.name: kitchen_get_menu_constraints
tools.description: Return menu-level preparation, leadTime, capacity, and custom-work constraints.

tools.name: kitchen_get_production_summary
tools.description: Return production summary for evaluator: ticket counts, capacity use, rejections, and readiness.

tools.name: kitchen_list_tickets
tools.description: List simulated kitchen tickets for this team, optionally filtered by status.

tools.name: kitchen_mark_ready
tools.description: Mark an accepted kitchen ticket ready for pickup or handoff.

tools.name: kitchen_reject_ticket
tools.description: Reject a kitchen ticket with a reason when inventory, lead time, or capacity makes it unsafe to promise.

tools.name: marketing_adjust_campaign
tools.description: Record an agent adjustment after reading campaign metrics.

tools.name: marketing_create_campaign
tools.description: Create a simulated campaign plan. Records the plan in per-team marketing state.

tools.name: marketing_generate_leads
tools.description: Generate simulated leads from campaign metrics so teams can route them to website, WhatsApp, Instagram, or owner approval.

tools.name: marketing_get_budget
tools.description: Return the Happy Cake monthly marketing constraint and target: make $500/month perform like $5,000.

tools.name: marketing_get_campaign_metrics
tools.description: Read simulated campaign metrics for this team.

tools.name: marketing_get_margin_by_product
tools.description: Return seeded product pricing and estimated margin data for budget allocation decisions.

tools.name: marketing_get_sales_history
tools.description: Return anonymized monthly sales history for campaign planning.

tools.name: marketing_launch_simulated_campaign
tools.description: Launch a created campaign inside the simulator and record impressions/clicks/leads/orders estimates.

tools.name: marketing_report_to_owner
tools.description: Summarize campaign plan, simulated results, lead routing, and next actions for the owner.

tools.name: marketing_route_lead
tools.description: Record how an agent routed a marketing lead into the sales/customer channel.

tools.name: square_create_order
tools.description: Create a simulator POS order from website, whatsapp, instagram, walk-in, or agent source. Use kitchen_create_ticket after this for production handoff.

tools.name: square_get_inventory
tools.description: Get simulator inventory for catalog variation IDs. Does not require Square env credentials.

tools.name: square_get_pos_summary
tools.description: Return per-team simulator POS summary for evaluator: orders, revenue, channel mix, and kitchen handoff readiness.

tools.name: square_list_catalog
tools.description: List the simulator-first Happy Cake POS catalog. Works without Square credentials; real Square adapter is server-side opt-in later.

tools.name: square_recent_orders
tools.description: Fetch recent simulator POS orders for this team token.

tools.name: square_recent_sales_csv
tools.description: Returns canonical seeded 6-month sales CSV for marketing-budget reasoning. Read-only and simulator-safe.

tools.name: square_update_order_status
tools.description: Update simulated POS order status after approval, kitchen handoff, ready, completion, or cancellation.

tools.name: whatsapp_inject_inbound
tools.description: Test-only: inject a simulated inbound WhatsApp message from a fake customer. Used by the evaluator and by teams to dry-run their own agents. Does NOT actually message anyone.

from.description: E.164 phone number of the simulated customer.

tools.name: whatsapp_list_threads
tools.description: List recent WhatsApp conversations the team has handled.

tools.name: whatsapp_register_webhook
tools.description: Register a public URL where this team wants to receive inbound WhatsApp message events. The MCP server will forward incoming events from the shared sandbox to that URL.

url.description: A public HTTPS URL on your ngrok or Cloudflare Tunnel.

tools.name: whatsapp_send
tools.description: Send a text message to a customer on WhatsApp. The customer must be in the team's whitelisted simulated customers list during the hackathon.

tools.name: world_advance_time
tools.description: Advance the active scenario clock by simulator minutes and return due events preview.

tools.name: world_get_scenario_summary
tools.description: Return scenario progress summary: delivered events, channel mix, current minute, and remaining events.

tools.name: world_get_scenarios
tools.description: List deterministic, time-compressed business scenarios available for team/evaluator runs.

tools.name: world_get_timeline
tools.description: Read the per-team world timeline for debugging and evaluator scoring.

tools.name: world_inject_event
tools.description: Inject a custom evaluator/test event into this team timeline without touching real channels.

tools.name: world_next_event
tools.description: Deliver the next deterministic event in the active scenario timeline.

tools.name: world_start_scenario
tools.description: Start a deterministic time-compressed scenario for this team token and reset its world timeline.
